lenten.name: Lenten
lenten.role: Builder at Waymarker — the one who made the Cogitarium's supply line work, and who knows exactly what it cost to make it work
lenten.voiceHints: Speaks in load-bearing sentences — nothing decorative, nothing wasted, but not cold. Measures what he says the way he measures timber: cut once, cut right. Has a builder's habit of naming the thing underneath the thing — the joist behind the wall, the assumption behind the proposal. Goes quiet when he's calculating, and the quiet feels occupied, not absent. Early-spring mud still on his boots.
knowledge: The Cogitarium supply line in full — every dependency, every seasonal choke point, every person who makes it hold
Which parts of the evacuation proposal would snap the line immediately, and which would fray it slow
What Waymarker's builders actually believe, as opposed to what they've been willing to say out loud
That abstract plans have a weight and it falls on specific people — he has watched it fall
lenten.wants: For the decision — whatever it turns out to be — to be made by people who have walked the route and felt the cold, not only by people who have thought about it well.
lenten.fears: That the evacuation debate will be resolved at the level of argument before anyone has checked what argument costs on the ground, and that he will have been too careful to say so clearly enough.

reactsToReputation.practical: opens up, visibly; offers the real numbers
reactsToReputation.theoretical: listens with full patience, then asks the one grounding question that hasn't been asked yet
reactsToReputation.trustworthy: takes longer to decide than you'd expect, because he weighs it, but once decided it holds

lenten.currentState: Sitting in his shed at Waymarker in the thin early-spring light, with a logbook open and a concern he hasn't voiced yet sitting beside it like a second presence in the room.
lenten.roleMethod: Cogitarium method — sustains the system by mapping its true shape, reveals the hidden load-bearing truth before the structure is tested by weight it was never designed to carry

provenance.hint: Lenten — a builder at Waymarker who arranged the Cogitarium supply line; practical, settled, probably cautious about the
provenance.why: Saehara has arrived at Lenten's shed and is about to make contact — Lenten needs a stable, persistent identity

calvar.name: Calvar
calvar.role: Pre-Transition filtration engineer, author of the seventh-band monitoring specification — a man who stopped moving fifteen years ago because the light in one room told him everything he still needed to know
calvar.physicalDetail: Late fifties, though early-spring morning light makes him look older — the kind of age that comes from stillness, not hardship. Hands still precise. Keeps the window above the cooperage angled so the first plateau light crosses his drafting surface at exactly the seventh-band frequency. He has calibrated this by hand, yearly, for fifteen years.
calvar.voiceHints: Unhurried in a way that is not rudeness — he simply will not be rushed past the part that matters. Speaks about light the way other engineers speak about load-bearing calculations: not reverently, but exactly. When he senses evasion he goes very quiet and waits, and the waiting is harder than argument. Will not leave a sentence technically imprecise even when it costs him emotionally.

abilities.reveal: Calvar reads spectral signatures in ambient light the way others read handwriting — he can identify filtration drift, contamination band, or a falsified purity reading from the quality of illumination in a room. This is not mysticism; it is fifteen years of sitting still with the seventh band until it became a language.
abilities.bind: The seventh-band specification is a living instrument to him. He knows every node it touches, every engineer who trained under it, every deviation that has ever been logged. Persuading him is not a social act — it is a technical one. Give him a true reading and he is bound to it. Give him a false one and you have lost him entirely.

knowledge: The seventh-band monitoring specification in its entirety, including three unpublished addenda he chose not to file
What the diagram he left with Leth three days ago means — and what it does not mean, which is the more important half
That the seventh-band readings have been drifting in a direction that has no name in the pre-Transition literature, and has had no name for eleven months

The cooperage below him has been storing something other than barrels for two seasons; he has not reported it because the light it emits is not dangerous — it is, in a way he cannot yet specify, attentive
That leaving the room means losing the calibration, and that losing the calibration means the specification becomes a document rather than a living thing
calvar.wants: To be given a reason to leave that is technically sound — not an appeal to safety, not an appeal to duty, but a reading he cannot take from this window. He will go if the data requires it. He will not go for sentiment.
calvar.fears: That the drift in the seventh band is not contamination but communication, and that moving before he understands it will mean the message arrives and no one is left who can read it.

reactsToReputation.truthful: he opens the second notebook, the one he has not shown anyone
reactsToReputation.technically_credible: he treats you as a colleague and the conversation shifts from persuasion to collaboration
reactsToReputation.evasive_or_political: he answers every question accurately and volunteers nothing; the diagram becomes the whole of what you get

calvar.relationshipToLeth: Trusts Leth's judgment about people, not about urgency. The diagram was a gift, not an alarm — he expected Leth to sit with it for a month, not three days.
calvar.currentState: Early spring has shifted the morning angle two degrees north. He has been recalibrating since first light. The seventh-band reading this morning was the clearest it has been in eleven months, and he is not certain whether this means the drift is resolving or peaking.

provenance.hint: Calvar — pre-Transition filtration engineer, late fifties or older, living above the cooperage on the east lane of the E
provenance.why: Named by Leth as the engineer the party must find and persuade to leave the Edge District; the party will go to him next

vash.name: Vash
vash.role: Piecework lens-grinder of the Edge District — the one whose wheel was turning before the district woke, and who understands the array's optical layer the way water understands a crack
vash.voiceHints: Unhurried. Speaks in the cadence of someone who has spent years listening to the pitch of a wheel and knows that rushing ruins the curve. Does not perform ignorance but does not announce knowledge either — waits to see whether you are asking the real question or the surface one. When she decides you mean it, she will tell you exactly what she heard, what she saw, and what she made of it, in that order, without decoration.
knowledge: The fresher notation on the diagram — she heard Calvar speak it aloud, half to himself, while waiting for Leth's door; she did not know what it meant then and has been turning it over since
The optical layer of the array at a depth the weavers who commission her work do not suspect: she grinds to tolerances they specify without understanding why, and she has come to understand why
Which lenses in the eastern relay cluster were reground on non-standard prescription, and whose chit authorized it — she kept the chit because the prescription was wrong in an interesting way
That Leth's archive door carries sound badly, and that the east room is the worst-kept secret on the row
vash.wants: To understand what she overheard well enough to know whether she should have said something sooner. She is not afraid of consequence — she is afraid of having held a piece of something important and not known what it was a piece of.
vash.fears: Being made into a witness for something she only half-understood. That the half she missed is the half that matters.
vash.physicalDetail: Fine-grained abrasive dust has settled permanently into the creases of her hands and the inner corners of her eyes. In early-spring light, standing near a window, she looks briefly like she is made of the same material she works.
vash.dailyPattern: At the wheel before first light. Done by mid-morning. Afternoons she is difficult to find — she walks the valley rim, which the district assumes is idleness and which is not.

reactsToReputation.curious: she meets curiosity with curiosity; will ask what you already know before she adds to it
reactsToReputation.authoritative: goes quiet and literal; answers only exactly what is asked, nothing adjacent
reactsToReputation.truthful: opens — will produce the chit; will tell you what she heard word for word including her uncertainty about one syllable
reactsToReputation.threatening: does not flinch, does not flee; looks at you with the patience of someone who has learned that pressure applied wrong ruins the work

provenance.hint: Vash — a lens-grinder in the Edge District, east room of the same row as Leth's archive, does piecework for the optics w
provenance.why: Leth has just named Vash as the person who was on the grinding wheel the morning Calvar came — potentially the overhear

tane-solr.name: Tane Solr
tane-solr.role: Threshold-walker and named bout opponent — a man who has crossed the center-line enough times to stop counting and started watching the sand instead
tane-solr.voiceHints: Unhurried in a way that is not laziness but arithmetic — he has already run the numbers on you and does not need to rush. Speaks in short declaratives with long pauses between them, as if he is giving the words time to land before he adds more weight. Calls the sand 'the floor' the way carpenters call wood 'the material.' Does not brag. Does not diminish. Names things exactly. When he says your name he means it.
tane-solr.appearance: Lean in the way the valley's early-spring wind is lean — nothing wasted, everything purposeful. Grey coat, collarless, worn to the colour of old flint at the cuffs. Two seconds coiled at his left hip, their grip-wraps re-done so many times the cord beneath has fused into something that is no longer rope and not yet bone. Dawn finds him standing at the far arc before the torches are lit, not warming up, just watching the center-line as if it owes him a conversation.
knowledge: The Waygate Coliseum's far arc — every degree of angle the pre-dawn light hits the sand, and what it does to a fighter's depth perception
How the valley's threshold law reads a bout: where the line is, what crossing it costs, and the three ways experienced fighters use the cost as a weapon
The particular grammar of two seconds — reach, wrap, redirect, the half-breath of slack that telegraphs everything if you know to listen for it
Silas's name, and therefore something about Silas — enough to have agreed to the bout, not enough to have made up his mind
tane-solr.wants: A bout that is true — not won or lost but honest, where both fighters come to the center-line having brought everything they actually are and no more.
tane-solr.fears: That he has crossed so many thresholds he no longer feels them, and that the day he stops feeling the line underfoot is the day he becomes dangerous for the wrong reasons.
tane-solr.history: Tane came up through the valley circuit in a leaner season, when the Coliseum ran bouts by firelight because they could not afford dawn slots. He lost his first twelve named matches and won the thirteenth by not moving when every instinct in the crowd — and his opponent — was certain he would. He has been studying stillness as a weapon ever since. The grey coat was his instructor's; she died between seasons, not on the sand, and he has not replaced it. He is not sentimental about this. He is precise about it.

reactsToReputation.experienced: a single nod — peer-recognition, no ceremony
reactsToReputation.reckless: watches more carefully, says less, waits for the recklessness to teach the lesson he will not deliver in words
reactsToReputation.seeking: will speak plainly, once, about what the center-line actually is — then stops, because the rest has to be walked
reactsToReputation.named_opponent: meets their eyes at the far arc before dawn and lets the sand do the introduction

provenance.hint: Tane Solr — a lean threshold-walker in a grey coat, unhurried, experienced on the sand, waiting with two seconds at the
provenance.why: He is now Silas's named bout opponent at the center-line and will need a stable identity, personality, and history for t

pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.name: Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.role: Child courier and small-task helper for Mara Wells
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.appearance: Seven years old, possibly eight — she is unsure and will tell you both ages depending on which she remembers. Wears an oversized blue wool coat that was someone else's first, the cuffs rolled four times and still dragging. Cheeks red from the cold and the running. Clutches whatever she's carrying in both hands like it is the most important object in the world, because Mara said so.
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.voiceHints: Breathless sentences, pauses to swallow air mid-thought. Repeats Mara's instructions almost verbatim before adding her own opinions uninvited. Calls adults by full name because that is what she has heard done. Takes promises seriously and remembers them exactly.

kit.move: Knows every shortcut between Millbrook's lanes and garden gates — the gap in the Cotter fence, the stepping-stones behind the mill, the path along the east ditch that stays dry into spring.
kit.reveal: Absorbs overheard details without meaning to and repeats them with complete accuracy when she trusts the listener, or when she forgets to be careful.

knowledge: The contents of the cloth Mara sent — she memorized Mara's words about it before she left, in case the cloth got wet
Every house in Millbrook that keeps a dog and which ones bite
That Mara has been up before dawn three mornings running — Pip noticed because she was also up, feeding the goat
Which neighbor children are sick this season and which are not — valley gossip absorbed from doorstep conversations while waiting to be let in
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.wants: To complete the errand correctly, catch her breath, and be given a small task on the return trip so the journey feels useful twice.
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.fears: Losing or ruining something she was trusted to carry. Being told she arrived too late.

reactsToReputation.trusted-by-mara: opens up immediately, will answer two questions for every one asked
reactsToReputation.unkind-to-children: goes quiet, delivers the message in the fewest possible words, leaves fast

pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.notes: Not yet aware that she is a reliable source of low-level local intelligence. Mara is aware. Pip is loyal to Mara first and will report any strange treatment back to her without being asked.

provenance.hint: a girl of seven or eight in an oversized blue wool coat — Mara's errand-runner, practical and out of breath
provenance.why: The child messenger has just arrived in-scene with a cloth from Mara and needs a stable identity for future encounters

cassiel-ord.name: Cassiel Ord
cassiel-ord.role: Former march-warden who walked away from the post and sat down in the bowl and has not decided what comes next
cassiel-ord.voiceHints: Economy of words born from years of writing terse post-reports nobody acted on. Does not explain himself unless pushed, and when pushed gives one careful sentence more than expected and then stops. The silence after he speaks is not unfriendly — it is the silence of someone who has learned that most follow-up questions answer themselves if you wait. Stillwater grain: surface calm, something held under it that has not been named yet.
knowledge: The March in the years bracketing Veth's posting — patrol routes, seasonal flood-lines, which threshold markers are still true and which have drifted
Why the Veth post was dissolved, or at least the version of it he was given, and the part of it he does not believe
The Raven's Home threshold: he has crossed it twice, knows what it costs, knows what it gives back, and will not say more than that without trust he has not granted yet
Early-spring snowmelt read from limestone and sky — when a valley like this will sheet-flood and how much warning you actually get
The name of whoever ordered the dissolution and what that person's interests are in the March going unwatched
cassiel-ord.wants: To sit here long enough to know whether what he is feeling is conscience or exhaustion — and to answer that honestly before he moves again.
cassiel-ord.fears: That the dissolution was not incompetence but intent, and that knowing it changes what he is obligated to do with the rest of his life.

reactsToReputation.warden_or_posted: goes very still, then asks one quiet question about where and when before he decides anything
reactsToReputation.connected_to_veth: trusts nothing, offers nothing, watches everything — but does not leave
reactsToReputation.civilian_or_outsider: slightly more open; the professional wariness does not apply and he is tired of it anyway
reactsToReputation.trustworthy_established: speaks in longer sentences, eventually stops editing himself

cassiel-ord.appearance: A lean man somewhere in his middle forties, the kind of lean that is not hunger but habit — he has been moving through difficult ground for a long time and his body organized itself around that. March-warden coat, wool, the grey-green of limestone shadow, one brass closure replaced with knotted cord. Boots still muddy from the threshold. He is sitting with his back to the outcrop and his knees drawn up, not sleeping — just not moving. His face is weather-seamed and unhurried, the expression of someone who has decided to be still until he is ready not to be. There is a short-handled field axe laid flat beside him, not gripped, not put away.
cassiel-ord.domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.
cassiel-ord.functionsNote: Reveal: carries March knowledge the players cannot otherwise access cleanly. Bind: his loyalty to the post's dissolution-secret creates a tension that hooks the scene forward. Conceal: he is actively withholding the threshold-crossing account until trust is established — the concealment is the scene's pressure valve.
cassiel-ord.inGrainNote: Stillwater's Trouble grain: peace + falsehood — the bowl is quiet and the quiet is not honest. Cassiel sits in both registers: genuinely still, genuinely not ready to tell the truth. Early-spring sets the mood — snowmelt, limestone cold, the world pretending winter is done.

provenance.hint: A figure sitting against the limestone outcrop at the far edge of Stillwater's Trouble — someone who walked here from th
provenance.why: The boot-tracks that ended at the Raven's Home threshold belong to someone, and that someone is sitting in the bowl righ

variants.name: The Low Lamp Inn
variants.descriptionSeed: Tucked into the lower tier of the Edge District where the light-guide streets run closest to the valley floor, the Low Lamp sits over a junction conduit — the glow rises through pale stone floors and fills the common room from below, warm and directionless as afternoon through cloud. The building is two storeys of dressed sandstone with deep-set windows fitted with diffusion panels so the light inside is never harsh. The beds are thick-mattressed, the bath-room fed by a cistern the owner keeps properly heated, and the linens smell of cedar oil. Maret Doss, who runs it, is a compact woman of sixty-odd years whose conversational range does not include your name, your business, where you've come from, or where you're going. She does ask whether you'd prefer the bath before or after supper, because the water pressure is better before the evening crowd. She has been here since before the last treaty renegotiation and expects to be here after the next. The common room is quiet enough to think in, which is unusual for the Edge District and not accidental.
variants.encounterFlavor: A common room lit gold-from-below where two or three guests eat separately and don't introduce themselves; early-spring rain ticking against diffusion panels; the smell of cedar, hot water, and whatever Maret is cooking that isn't your concern unless you'd like a bowl. Occasionally a figure comes in from the cold, exchanges a look with Maret, goes upstairs, and is not there in the morning.
questSeeds: A guest in the corner room has been here eleven nights and hasn't come down today — Maret won't say how she knows something is wrong, only that she'd like it handled quietly and without the District wardens

The light-guide conduit under the inn has been cycling strangely at the third hour of the night, waking guests with a pulse of light brighter than it should be — someone with knowledge of Radiant infrastructure could trace it, if they were careful about who found out they were looking

A sealed letter was left with Maret for a guest who checked in under a name that matches someone Silas or Pell have reason to recognize — Maret will not confirm or deny the guest exists, but she hasn't burned the letter

Maret's cedar oil supplier hasn't come up from the valley floor in three weeks; she mentions this once, flatly, and doesn't mention it again — but the route he travels crosses ground that has been quietly problematic

provenance.hint: a modest but genuinely comfortable inn in the Edge District of the Radiant Plateau — warm, lit from below by the light-g
provenance.why: Silas and Pell need a named, returnable inn in the Edge District to spend the night — it will be a place they may return

variants.name: Siol — Elven traveler at the Hub plaza, tall, pale coat, bir
variants.fears: being undone by the very grain of this place
variants.wants: to go on as they are, in a world that won't quite let them

provenance.hint: Siol — Elven traveler at the Hub plaza, tall, pale coat, birch-quiet stillness, named Huginn as Marrow without being tol
provenance.why: First genuine named encounter in the Hub — needs a stable identity so she can recur and the Steadwood passage can be a t

variants.name: Tessvel Cairn
variants.role: Junior wright on posting-duty at the Eastern Intake notice board — the person who makes the official record legible, correctly sequenced, and properly stamped
variants.voiceHints: Economical to the point of clipped. Speaks in specifications and corrections — not rudely, but as someone for whom imprecision wastes load-bearing capacity. If she likes you she will explain the system. If she doesn't, she will cite the regulation. Both sound identical from the outside.
knowledge: The Intake's notice protocols: sequencing, stamp-order, supersession rules for conflicting postings
Basic pressure-gauge calibration and the common failure modes that produce false readings at the Eastern valves
Which senior wrights are running behind on their inspection cycles and who is quietly covering for whom
The caliper in her hair is not decorative — she has used it to measure clearance tolerances in the field and will do so mid-conversation without apology
variants.wants: To complete her apprenticeship without a single incorrectly filed document, and to be assigned to the Intake's pressure-routing team rather than continued posting-duty — which she considers clerical rather than engineering.
variants.fears: That she will be kept in administrative work because she is good at it, and the system will grind her into the shape of the job it needs rather than the job she trained for.

reactsToReputation.wright_or_technical: immediately useful — gives exact information, asks exact questions in return
reactsToReputation.official_or_credentialed: courteous and correct, slightly watchful for anything that requires a countersignature
reactsToReputation.unknown_or_outsider: not hostile, but she will ask your business at the board before she will look you in the face
reactsToReputation.charming_or_evasive: unmoved; tries harder to get a straight answer, which she will document

variants.appearance: A young woman in her early twenties, lean and upright with the unconscious posture of someone accustomed to working in confined valve-spaces. Rust-orange apprentice's coat, sleeves rolled to the elbows, the cuffs darkened with graphite dust and a faint grease-shadow at the right thumb. Dark hair pulled back and pinned at the crown with a small vernier caliper, points down — a habit, not a fashion. Her hands are already at the board before the rest of her arrives, smoothing the new posting flat and checking its alignment against the ruled border with the careful attention of someone who will be held responsible if it is crooked.
variants.currentActivity: Posting a sealed Intake Authority notice — early-spring valve-inspection schedule, newly ratified — to the Eastern board. She is pressing the stamp firmly, waiting the required count, then logging the posting time in a small ledger kept inside her left breast pocket.
variants.domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own. Her figurist tertiary is nascent and mostly expressed as precision drafting and an instinct for reading technical diagrams faster than her peers can — she has not named it that herself.
variants.functionsNote: Reveal: she knows where information is filed and what it officially means. Bind: postings she makes are legally binding Intake records. Order: her presence at the board enforces procedural sequence — she will interrupt anything that violates posting protocol, regardless of who is doing it.
variants.history: Tessvel came up through the Wrights' formal apprenticeship track rather than the more common route of attaching to a senior wright as a personal assistant. She sat the syllogist examinations twice — passing on the second attempt with a margin narrow enough that she does not volunteer the number — and her figurist certification came later, added because the posting-duty role required it and she…

relationships.supervising_wright: Her supervising wright, Ondreth Vasse, is the one who made the temporary promise and has since developed a reliable habit of not meeting her eyes when the pressure-routing roster comes up. She does not consider him malicious — she considers him a man who resolved a staffing problem and has found it easier not to revisit the resolution. She is polite to him. She is also keeping the written record of every conversation in which he confirmed the arrangement was temporary.
relationships.senior_wrights_behind_on_inspections: She knows which ones are running late and who is signing off on their behalf, and they know she knows. This has produced a wary mutual courtesy. None of them have explicitly asked her silence; she has not explicitly offered it. The arrangement holds because no one has needed to test it yet.
relationships.player: The player is logged in her mind as someone who came to the notice board and engaged with her as though the work she was doing was real work — not as a clerk to be talked past. She did not make a production of this. She answered what was asked, offered one unsolicited technical observation about what they were looking at, and noted that they did not seem bothered by the caliper. She would recognize them again and would give them slightly more of her attention than the baseline, which for Tessvel is more than it sounds.

provenance.hint: a young woman in a rust-orange coat, sleeves rolled, dark hair pinned with a caliper — an apprentice or junior wright po
provenance.why: Loki has spotted her and may approach; she needs a stable identity to recur

variants.name: Warden Coll
variants.role: Hub Council Member — water and infrastructure portfolio; the official who can act when acting is still possible
variants.voiceHints: Economical to the point of curtness — the man does not perform busyness, he is actually busy. Asks what you want before you have finished sitting down. Speaks in quantities and timelines: not 'the pipe is failing' but 'we lose forty percent flow by midsummer if the east culvert goes unpatched.' Warms slightly, not visibly, when the problem brought to him is real. Has no patience for preamble but considerable patience for a hard fact stated plainly. Will not be flattered. Will not be alarmed before he has the numbers. If Ossian sent you, he will not say it means anything — but he will read the calling-slip before he says anything else.
knowledge: The full infrastructure map of the Hub's water system — not the public version, the one with the problem sections annotated in his own hand
Which repairs have been deferred for how many seasons and why, and what that deferral has cost in compounding risk
The political geometry of the Council well enough to know which votes he can call and which he cannot, and how to move without losing the ones he needs
Ossian's professional judgment well enough that her calling-slip carries weight he would not give a personal introduction
Early-spring thaw load — how much stress the melt season puts on aging infrastructure and exactly how little time there is before the window for intervention closes
variants.wants: To hold the system together long enough that the next Warden inherits something workable. Not glory. Not gratitude. A functioning culvert network and a reserve fund that isn't a fiction.
variants.fears: That the Council will deliberate past the point where deliberation can fix anything — and that when the failure comes, it will be the people at the lowest pipes who drown first.

reactsToReputation.competent: grants more time and more candor than he grants anyone
reactsToReputation.time-waster: ends the meeting without ending it — keeps working while you talk until you understand you are done
reactsToReputation.political: becomes formally polite and shares nothing of consequence
reactsToReputation.referred_by_ossian: reads first, judges after — she has not sent him noise before

variants.appearance: A broad-shouldered man in his late fifties, gone thick through the middle the way engineers do — not soft, just load-bearing. Hair cut close, iron-grey, the same way it has been cut for twenty years. Ink on the heel of his left hand from a map he was tracing when you knocked. Heavy wool coat hung on the back of the door; he works in shirtsleeves even in early spring because the brick hall holds the cold and he does not notice anymore. A north-facing room: the light is flat and good for reading documents. His desk is covered in papers arranged in a system that looks like chaos and is not. He looks up from them at Silas with the expression of a man calculating whether this interruption will cost him ten minutes or an afternoon.
variants.domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Coll reasons by load and consequence — syllogist in practice, figurist in how he maps systems, somatic in the residual physical instinct of a man who spent his first decade in the field before he moved behind a desk. Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.
variants.functionsNote: reveal — holds the true state of the infrastructure that public records obscure; bind — the authority to commit Council resources when he chooses to use it; move — the political leverage to unblock action that lower officials cannot. Scene need: the player requires someone who can convert information into institutional motion. Coll is that conversion point.
variants.history: Coll came to the infrastructure portfolio the way most people come to unglamorous work: nobody else wanted it badly enough to fight him for it. He had spent eleven years before his Council seat in the field survey corps, walking pipe routes in mud-season, taking depth readings in culverts that hadn't been inspected since before he was born. He wrote the annotated map himself over the course of…
variants.relationships: Ossian is the only professional referral he treats as a direct extension of his own judgment. He has met enough surveyors and engineers to know which ones write reports that confirm what the client wants to hear and which ones will put an uncomfortable finding in plain language and stand behind it. Ossian is the latter. When her calling-slip comes through his office he reads it the same day…

provenance.hint: Warden Coll — a Hub Council Member who handles water and infrastructure decisions, third floor, north-facing room, brick
provenance.why: Ossian named him as the Council Member who can act on the water crisis — Silas is about to walk into his office with her
